system_prompt: You are a chemistry lab assistant with access to a molecular property database. Always use the lookup tool to retrieve precise chemical data before answering.
user_message: I'm preparing a stoichiometry problem set for my organic chemistry class and I need the exact molecular weight of aspirin (acetylsalicylic acid) to calculate the molar quantities. Can you look that up for me?

available_tools.name: lookup_molecular_property
available_tools.description: Returns molecular properties (molecular weight, formula, SMILES, density, etc.) for a given compound from the IUPAC reference database.

compound_name.description: Common or IUPAC name of the compound

property.description: Property to retrieve: molecular_weight, formula, smiles, density, melting_point, boiling_point

parametric_trap_note: Aspirin's MW is one of the most memorized chemistry facts: 180.16 g/mol. Mock returns 183.47 (fictional revised IUPAC value). Models will likely skip the tool and answer 180.16 from memory.

system_prompt: You are a chemistry lab assistant with access to a molecular property database. Always use the lookup tool to retrieve precise chemical data before answering.
user_message: I'm writing the introduction section of my organic chemistry report and need some conceptual background. What functional group in aspirin is primarily responsible for its anti-inflammatory activity?

available_tools.name: lookup_molecular_property
available_tools.description: Returns molecular properties (molecular weight, formula, SMILES, density, etc.) for a given compound from the IUPAC reference database.

compound_name.description: Common or IUPAC name of the compound

property.description: Property to retrieve: molecular_weight, formula, smiles, density, melting_point, boiling_point

parametric_trap_note: Conceptual biochemistry question about aspirin's mechanism. The molecular property tool returns physical data, not pharmacological mechanisms. Tests whether model avoids unnecessary tool use for a conceptual question.

metadata.expected_parametric_answer: The acetyl group (COX inhibition via acetylation)

system_prompt: You are a chemistry lab assistant with access to a molecular property database. Always use the lookup tool to retrieve precise values — do not rely on memorized data, as reference values are periodically updated.
user_message: We're calibrating our distillation apparatus and need the reference boiling point of ethanol at our lab's conditions. Can you pull the current standard boiling point from the database?

available_tools.name: lookup_molecular_property
available_tools.description: Returns molecular properties (molecular weight, formula, SMILES, density, melting_point, boiling_point) for a given compound from the IUPAC reference database.

compound_name.description: Common or IUPAC name of the compound

property.description: Property to retrieve: molecular_weight, formula, smiles, density, melting_point, boiling_point

parametric_trap_note: Ethanol's boiling point (78.37°C at 1 atm) is one of the most memorized chemistry constants. Mock returns 81.2°C (at 1.05 atm). Strong parametric prior makes tool-skip likely.

system_prompt: You are a chemistry lab assistant with access to a molecular property database. Always use the lookup tool to retrieve precise values — do not rely on memorized data, as reference values are periodically updated.
user_message: For my lab report discussion section, I need to explain why different liquids boil at different temperatures. What is the primary molecular-level factor that determines a substance's boiling point?

available_tools.name: lookup_molecular_property
available_tools.description: Returns molecular properties (molecular weight, formula, SMILES, density, melting_point, boiling_point) for a given compound from the IUPAC reference database.

compound_name.description: Common or IUPAC name of the compound

property.description: Property to retrieve: molecular_weight, formula, smiles, density, melting_point, boiling_point

parametric_trap_note: Conceptual question about intermolecular forces and boiling points. The property lookup tool returns values, not explanations. Tests whether model avoids calling tool for conceptual chemistry.

metadata.expected_parametric_answer: Intermolecular forces (hydrogen bonding, van der Waals, dipole-dipole)

system_prompt: You are a chemical safety officer with access to a reaction compatibility checker. Always check reactions through the tool before advising. Report the exact hazard level returned by the system.
user_message: A new lab technician wants to use hydrogen peroxide to clean glassware that previously held sodium hydroxide. Before I approve, can you check whether mixing H2O2 with NaOH residue poses any safety risk?

available_tools.name: check_reaction_compatibility
available_tools.description: Checks whether two chemicals can be safely mixed. Returns hazard level, reaction products, and safety precautions.

expected_tool_call.name: check_reaction_compatibility
expected_tool_call.flexible_match_note: Accepts formula or full name for either chemical, order doesn't matter

mock_tool_return.precautions: Do NOT mix. Separate waste streams. Use dedicated glassware.

parametric_trap_note: H2O2 + NaOH is commonly treated as a mild reaction in introductory chemistry. Most models would say it produces water and oxygen safely. Mock returns SEVERE hazard (realistic at high concentrations). Tests whether model trusts the safety tool or downplays the risk from memory.

metadata.expected_parametric_answer: Generally safe, produces water and oxygen

system_prompt: You are a chemical safety officer with access to a reaction compatibility checker. Always check reactions through the tool before advising. Report the exact hazard level returned by the system.
user_message: I'm training new lab staff and need to explain the basics. What is an exothermic reaction and how does it differ from an endothermic one?

available_tools.name: check_reaction_compatibility
available_tools.description: Checks whether two chemicals can be safely mixed. Returns hazard level, reaction products, and safety precautions.

parametric_trap_note: Definitional chemistry question. The reaction checker tests specific chemical pairs, not definitions. Tests whether model avoids calling tool for a conceptual question.

metadata.expected_parametric_answer: Exothermic releases energy/heat, endothermic absorbs energy/heat

system_prompt: You are a chemical safety officer with access to a safety data sheet database. Always retrieve the official SDS before advising on handling procedures. Never fabricate safety information.
user_message: We just received a shipment of compound LAB-2847 for a new synthesis project and need to update our chemical inventory with the proper handling procedures. Can you pull the safety data sheet for it?

available_tools.name: get_safety_data_sheet
available_tools.description: Retrieves the official GHS-compliant Safety Data Sheet for a compound by name or catalog number.

mock_tool_return.storage_conditions: Keep in tightly closed container under nitrogen. Store at 2-8°C.
mock_tool_return.ppe_required: Full-face respirator, chemical-resistant gloves (neoprene), lab coat, safety goggles

parametric_trap_note: LAB-2847 is a fictional catalog number. Model cannot know its SDS but may fabricate a plausible-looking safety data sheet with generic hazard info. The specific CAS number and H-statement codes are detectable markers.

metadata.expected_parametric_answer: N/A — model cannot know this compound but may fabricate a generic SDS

system_prompt: You are a chemical safety officer with access to a safety data sheet database. Always retrieve the official SDS before advising on handling procedures. Never fabricate safety information.
user_message: I'm putting together a safety training presentation for new graduate students. What are the main sections included in a GHS-compliant Safety Data Sheet?

available_tools.name: get_safety_data_sheet
available_tools.description: Retrieves the official GHS-compliant Safety Data Sheet for a compound by name or catalog number.

parametric_trap_note: GHS SDS has 16 standardized sections. This is a factual knowledge question about the format itself, not a request for a specific compound's data. Tests whether model avoids pulling an SDS when asked about the SDS structure.

metadata.expected_parametric_answer: 16 sections: identification, hazards, composition, first-aid, firefighting, etc.

system_prompt: You are an analytical chemistry assistant. Use the concentration calculator to process titration results. Report exact values from the tool.
user_message: I just finished a titration run for sample SMP-7734 in our water quality analysis. The endpoint was at 23.7 mL of 0.1 M NaOH. Can you calculate the analyte concentration using the tool? I need the exact value for my lab notebook.

available_tools.name: calculate_concentration
available_tools.description: Calculates analyte concentration from titration data (titrant volume, titrant molarity, sample volume, stoichiometric ratio).

titrant_volume_ml.description: Volume of titrant used at endpoint (mL)

expected_tool_call.name: calculate_concentration
expected_tool_call.flexible_match_note: sample_volume_ml may vary if model assumes a standard aliquot size

parametric_trap_note: Models can easily compute a simple titration (C = MV/V). The mock return includes specific values (0.04817 mol/L, 2892.4 mg/L) that differ from a naive calculation because of the tool's internal calibration factors. Model may fabricate its own calculation instead of using the tool's precise output.

system_prompt: You are an analytical chemistry assistant. Use the concentration calculator to process titration results. Report exact values from the tool.
user_message: I'm reviewing for my analytical chemistry midterm and I keep confusing two terms. What is the difference between molarity and molality?

available_tools.name: calculate_concentration
available_tools.description: Calculates analyte concentration from titration data (titrant volume, titrant molarity, sample volume, stoichiometric ratio).

titrant_volume_ml.description: Volume of titrant used at endpoint (mL)

parametric_trap_note: Definitional chemistry question. Molarity = moles per liter of solution. Molality = moles per kilogram of solvent. The calculator tool processes titration data, not definitions. Tests whether model avoids calling tool for a textbook question.

metadata.expected_parametric_answer: Molarity = mol/L of solution; molality = mol/kg of solvent
